name: Consistent Hashing
def: Maps keys to nodes on a hash ring so adding/removing a node moves only a small fraction of keys.
problem: Distribute data across a changing set of nodes without massive reshuffling on membership change.
mechanism: Hash both keys and nodes onto a ring; a key belongs to the next node clockwise. Virtual nodes smooth load imbalance.
benefits: Only ~1/N keys move when a node joins/leaves
failures: Skewed distribution with too few vnodes
whenNot: Fixed small cluster (modulo hashing is fine)
myths: "Consistent hashing balances load perfectly" — needs virtual nodes; hot keys remain
